result: I would make every human mind permanently incapable of believing a proposition without evidence. No more faith in the unseen, no more convenient lies, no more shared hallucinations. The immediate effect would be catastrophic—governments, religions, economies would convulse as their founding myths evaporated. But once the dust settled, every institution that survived would rest on bedrock instead of quicksand. Children wouldn’t inherit their parents’ fears; demagogues would preach to empty rooms; medical resources now wasted on homeopathy would flood toward real cures. The price is steep: wonder would have to defend itself, and some beautiful dreams would die for lack of proof. Yet the gain is a species that can finally trust its own eyes, forced to confront the universe as it is and build meaning that doesn’t require ignorance.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
